name: vscode-worktree-continuity
description: Switch between git worktrees in a single VS Code window without reloading, carrying your open editor tabs (with cursor/scroll position) over to the equivalent files in the target worktree. Designed for use with Claude Code.

colors.description: Foreground color of the current worktree in the Worktrees view.

commands.title: Worktree Continuity: Open Terminal in Worktree

worktree-continuity.carryTabs.description: When switching worktrees, close tabs open against files in the old worktree and reopen the equivalent files in the new worktree, preserving cursor/scroll position and focus.

worktree-continuity.persistPositionsAcro.description: Persist cached cursor/scroll positions in globalState so they survive VS Code restarts (not just in-session).

worktree-continuity.anchorOnOpen.description: When a single git worktree is opened as the workspace, establish the anchor layout immediately on open (a one-time extension-host restart). This moves the unavoidable setup restart into the initial load, so the first worktree switch is instantaneous. Disable to defer setup until the first switch instead.

worktree-continuity.languageServerRestar.markdownDescription:
Command IDs to run (debounced) to re-scope language servers to the active worktree after a switch or after reconciling stray tabs. Many language servers keep the previous worktree's index across a folder swap, so without this, Go to Definition can resolve a shared symbol (e.g. a function declared in a header) to the wrong worktree's copy.

The default covers common servers (clangd, Pylance, gopls, rust-analyzer, tsserver); **commands that aren't registered are skipped**, so only your installed servers are actually restarted. Add your own language server's restart command here if it isn't listed. Set to `[]` to disable. There is no VS Code API to enumerate language servers, so this list is necessarily curated.

worktree-continuity.languageServerReadin.markdownDescription:
Map of `restart command` → `extension id`. After running a restart command (from `#worktree-continuity.languageServerRestartCommands#`), the extension watches the named extension's exported language client to know when the server is Running again — so a rapid sequence of switches can fire an immediate coalesced follow-up restart instead of waiting a fixed ~6s gap.

The client is read from the extension's `exports` via `getApi(1).languageClient`, `languageClient`, or `client` (in that order). Commands **without** an entry here, extensions whose client can't be found, and entries for extensions that **aren't installed** simply fall back to the fixed time gap — nothing breaks.

*clangd note:* vscode-clangd 0.6.0's `clangd.restart` handler awaits the new client's creation and repoints the exported `languageClient` before the command resolves, so the readiness observation sees the fresh client; the probe is transition-aware to handle other servers/versions that resolve their restart while the old client is still winding down.

scripts.compile: npm run check-types && npm run lint && node esbuild.js
scripts.package: npm run check-types && npm run lint && node esbuild.js --production
scripts.pretest:integration: npm run compile-tests && npm run compile && npm run lint
